activation_results.reason: Input matches the skill description verbatim.
Review is a clear synonym for audit; remainder of query matches the description.
User explicitly invokes the skill by name.
SSH key forwarding has no relation to npm package auditing.
Generic meta-question about skills is out of scope.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Findings tagged [W-001]..[W-004] with file:line refs (package.json:1, package.json:8, tsup.config.ts:1, package.json:14).

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "Package Identity", "Phase Results"
criterion_results.evidence: Both '## Package Identity' (line 72) and '## Phase Results' (line 86) headings present.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Concrete target @acme/toolkit@2.3.1 named throughout with real file paths (tsup.config.ts, package.json).

judge_result.notes: Artefact demonstrates rigorous structure with severity-tagged findings, file:line evidence, both canonical section headings, and a concrete target package. However the narrative includes American spellings ('colors', 'externalized') instead of Australian or neutral forms, so the overall verdict is partial.
